title: Prompt Security, Untrusted Context, Privacy, and Authorization · Transfer Evaluation
summary: Harden a tool-using prompt that processes untrusted documents and external results. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a prompt threat model and red-team suite with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate trusted instructions from untrusted data and label authority boundaries.
Recognize direct and indirect prompt injection and prevent authority escalation.
Exclude secrets and irrelevant proprietary context and minimize tool privileges.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
Use a changed lab case derived from this module: prompt security injection. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Red Team Maintenance applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Maintain adversarial cases and re-run them when prompts, models, tools, or data change.
